evidence: "One of the Best and Most Important Documentaries I've Seen in a Long Time!"
"The film runs close to 2 hours and there is about 15 minutes of footage that could have been left on the cutting room floor."
"The story was interesting for the first half of the film, but I felt like it really dragged around the halfway point and really wanted it to wrap things up."
"The film runs close to 2 hours and there is about 15 minutes of footage that could have been left on the cutting room floor."
"It feels like the writers this season took some early drafts for episodes that had been sitting on the shelf for a few years instead of coming up with new ideas."
"The only real complaint I have about is that it doesn't go into a whole lot of detail about the NSA and what he was fighting against..."
"The main reason why the film fails is because of its script."
"...the director does an excellent job of crafting the film with a tone that's comparable to the works of David Fincher..."
"...the film is very much a Dramedy and does a good job of keeping the tone balanced delivering a fair dose of laughs along with dramatic tearjerking moments..."
"...the cinematography and one of the best soundtracks I've heard in recent memory."
"...it's a truly inspiring (and important) true story and one of the years best films."
"...the film is absolutely hilarious! It never gets quite as raunchy as one might expect, but that doesn't make it any less hilarious."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for documentaries and comedies, particularly those with high production quality and emotional depth. They appreciate the craftsmanship of well-known directors like Martin Scorsese and David Fincher, while favoring slower-paced narratives that allow for character development and emotional engagement.
